channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Saskatoon Hosts Its First Aviation Day for Children on the Autism Spectrum
item.description: On Saturday morning, families gathered at the Saskatoon John G. Diefenbaker International Airport for the city’s first Autism Aviation Day. This initiative, hosted by Air Canada in partnership with Au...

item.title: Floods Kill 18 Across Kenya as Tana River and Eastern Region Face Highest Risk
item.description: The number of people who have died due to ongoing floods in Kenya has reached 18, according to the Ministry of Interior and National Administration. Most of these deaths have been caused by drowning a...

item.title: Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak Aboard Netherlands Cruise Ship Claims Three Lives
item.description: Three passengers aboard the Netherlands-based cruise ship MV Hondius have died, and three others are reported ill due to a suspected hantavirus outbreak. The ship, operated by Oceanwide Expeditions, w...

item.title: Russia’s latest drone and missile strikes kill 10 across Ukraine
item.description: Russian strikes have killed at least 10 people and injured 76 across several Ukrainian cities, according to officials. The attacks targeted Kherson, Odesa, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, and Sumy regions. In ...

item.title: Pakistan and China Enhance Cooperation in Economy, Industry, and Defence
item.description: President Asif Zardari’s week-long visit to China marks a strategic effort by Pakistan to focus its long-standing partnership with China on concrete economic and industrial priorities. The trip emphas...

item.title: Migrants in Australia face long delays and barriers in accessing mental health care, report finds
item.description: A new report from Mental Health Australia highlights significant delays and barriers faced by migrants and culturally diverse communities when seeking mental health support. The research, conducted th...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Regional Economic Ties with Kagame and Ruto Visits
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania is stepping up as a key hub for East African economic integration following back-to-back visits by President Paul Kagame of Rwanda and President William Ruto of Kenya. Analyst...

item.title: ACT public school staff overwhelmingly vote for strike action amid stalled negotiations
item.description: Teachers and school assistants across ACT public schools have voted overwhelmingly in favour of strike action following prolonged negotiations with the Education Directorate. Over 95 per cent of staff...

item.title: Evidence-Based Benefits of Magnesium and Creatine During Menopause
item.description: Menopause can bring a variety of challenging symptoms, including hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, anxiety, brain fog, and changes in muscle and bone health due to declining estrogen levels...

item.title: Rwanda-Tanzania Relations Strengthened Through Strategic Presidential Visit
item.description: President Paul Kagame made a one-day working visit to Dar es Salaam on May 3, 2026, showing the growing strategic partnership between Rwanda and Tanzania. During the visit, Kagame met with Tanzanian P...

item.title: Richarlison’s Header Lifts Tottenham Past Aston Villa and Out of Relegation Zone
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur secured a crucial 2-1 victory over a lackluster Aston Villa at Villa Park, with Richarlison’s header doubling their lead on the 25th minute. The win, Tottenham's second consecutive P...

item.title: MK Party to contest key municipalities in KZN, including eThekwini and Umngeni
item.description: The Umkhonto weSizwe Party (MKP) has announced its plans to contest several strategic municipalities in KwaZulu-Natal during the upcoming local government elections. Key targets include the DA-control...

item.title: The US Empire’s Decline and Australia’s Lack of Response
item.description: The article examines the accelerating decline of the United States as a global military and political power, highlighting recent conflicts and criticisms involving NATO allies. It details former Presi...

item.title: Kang'ata Announces Departure from UDA Party Ahead of 2027 Elections, Citing Policy Differences
item.description: Murang'a Governor Irungu Kang'ata has announced that he will not seek re-election under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) party, citing disagreements over key policy areas such as manufacturing, he...

item.title: Zacks Research Projects Q1 2026 Earnings for Diamondback Energy (FANG)
item.description: Zacks Research has issued its Q1 2026 earnings estimate for Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ:FANG), forecasting the oil and natural gas company to earn $3.50 per share for the quarter. The firm also m...

item.title: Tanzanians Rate Their Media as Most Free in Africa, Says Afrobarometer Survey
item.description: According to the recent Afrobarometer survey, majority of Tanzanians feel their media works freely without government interference, making Tanzania the top country in Africa for perceived press freedo...

item.title: Hamilton man faces multiple charges after injuring himself during attempted home invasion
item.description: Hamilton police have laid 19 charges against a 38-year-old man following an alleged attempted home invasion that resulted in the suspect shooting himself. Officers responded to reports of a shooting n...

item.title: Nsukka District Shows Strong Support for Tinubu and Mbah Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The people and leaders of Enugu North Senatorial District, which includes Nsukka Zone, have officially endorsed President Bola Tinubu and Governor Peter Mbah for the 2027 elections. The endorsement, m...

item.title: Rising harassment of Christians in Jerusalem highlighted after nun assault
item.description: Christians in Jerusalem are expressing serious concern after a widely circulated video showed a Catholic nun being attacked by a Jewish extremist. The incident, which took place near the Old City, sho...

item.title: Student Robbed While Returning Home from Job Hunting in Antipolo
item.description: A student returning home after searching for a job in Antipolo, Rizal, was reportedly robbed by unidentified suspects. The incident has raised concerns about safety in the area, particularly for young...
